title: Dakshina Mookambika Temple, North Paravur
tagline: Dakshina Mookambika Temple, North Paravur — spiritual town of Kerala

heroImage.alt: Dakshina Mookambika Temple, North Paravur, Kerala

overview.short: The Dakshina Mookambika Temple is a famous Mookambika temple in the town of North Paravur in the Ernakulam district of Kerala.
overview.description: The Dakshina Mookambika Temple is a famous Mookambika temple in the town of North Paravur in the Ernakulam district of Kerala. The presiding deity in this temple is Mookambika and sub-deities include Ganapathy, Kartikeya, Mahavishnu, Yakshi, Hanuman and Veerabhadran. A shrine devoted to Yakshi is at the southwest corner. The sanctum sanctorum is in the midst of a lotus pool.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: North Paravur
topPlaces.description: North Paravur (IPA: [vɐɖɐkːɐn pɐrɐʋuːr]; commonly known as Paravur or Parur, is a municipality is a major town and municipality in the northwestern part of the Kochi metropolitan area in the Indian state of Kerala. Situated near the confluence of the Periyar River, Chalakudy River, and the Arabian Sea, it serves as an important residential, commercial, and administrative centre in Ernakulam district.

topPlaces.name: St. Thomas Jacobite Syrian Church, North Paravur
topPlaces.description: Saint Thomas Jacobite Syrian church, also known as Paravur Cheriyapally, is a Syrian Orthodox church located in North Paravur, India. This church was constructed in AD 1566 in the midst of the seven bazaars of Paravur town by the parishioners of the ancient Kottakkavu Paravur Valiapally.

image.alt: St. Thomas Jacobite Syrian Church, North Paravur

topPlaces.name: Don Bosco Church, North Paravur
topPlaces.description: Don Bosco Church at North Paravur, Kerala, is a church of Paravur Parish under the Roman Catholic (Latin) Diocese of Kottappuram. Situated near Perumpadanna Junction at Paravur-Cherai road, this is one of the few churches in India named after Don Bosco.

topPlaces.name: Sree Venugopla Krishna Swami Devasthan
topPlaces.description: Sree Venugopla Krishna Swami Devasthan is a Hindu temple located in Chendamangalam, Kerala, India. It was established in 1900 at Chendamangalam (earlier known as Jayantha mangalam), 42 km from Ernakulam, 22 km from Aluva and 5 km from North Paravur.

topPlaces.name: Pattanam
topPlaces.description: Pattanam (Malayalam: [pɐʈːɐɳɐm]) is a village located in Paravur taluk, Ernakulam district, in the southern Indian state of Kerala. It is situated 2–3 km northwest of North Paravur, 3 km west of Chendamangalam, and 30 km north of Kochi (Cochin).

topPlaces.name: Manjaly
topPlaces.description: Manjali is a small town in Ernakulam district, Kerala, India situated near North Paravur. The Manjaly is also famous for Halva and Biryani.

topPlaces.name: Koottukad
topPlaces.description: Koottukad is a small village in Parur Taluk, Ernakulam district of Kerala state, South India.

topPlaces.name: Paravur taluk
topPlaces.description: Paravur Taluk, IPA: [pɐrɐʋuːr], is a taluk of Ernakulam District in the Indian State of Kerala. North Paravur is the capital of the taluk.

items.note: North Paravur (IPA: [vɐɖɐkːɐn pɐrɐʋuːr]; commonly known as Paravur or Parur, is a municipality is a major town and municipality in the northwestern part of the Kochi metropolitan area in the Indian state of Kerala. Situated near the confluence of the Periyar River, Chalakudy River, and the Arabian Sea, it serves as an important residential, commercial, and administrative centre in Ernakulam district.

This church was constructed in AD 1566 in the midst of the seven bazaars of Paravur town by the parishioners of the ancient Kottakkavu Paravur Valiapally.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dakshina Mookambika Temple, North Paravur?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dakshina Mookambika Temple, North Paravur is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dakshina Mookambika Temple, North Paravur?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (19 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (21 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dakshina Mookambika Temple, North Paravur?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dakshina Mookambika Temple, North Paravur at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dakshina Mookambika Temple, North Paravur?
faq.a: Stays in Dakshina Mookambika Temple, North Paravur start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dakshina Mookambika Temple, North Paravur?
faq.a: The top attractions are North Paravur, St. Thomas Jacobite Syrian Church, North Paravur, Don Bosco Church, North Paravur, Sree Venugopla Krishna Swami Devasthan, Pattanam.
